title: Fresh Lemonade
tagline: The real thing. No powder, no shortcuts.
description: Simple syrup-based lemonade that's perfectly balanced between sweet and tart. The only lemonade recipe you need.
steps: Make simple syrup: Heat 1 cup water and sugar in a saucepan, stirring until sugar dissolves. Let cool.
Juice the lemons. Strain out seeds and pulp (or leave some pulp for texture).
Combine simple syrup, lemon juice, and 6 cups cold water in a large pitcher.
Taste and adjust: add more water if too sweet, more syrup if too tart.
Refrigerate until cold, or serve immediately over lots of ice.
Garnish with lemon slices and mint.
notes: Room temperature lemons yield more juice. Roll them on the counter before cutting.
Simple syrup dissolves better than granulated sugar in cold liquid.
For pink lemonade: add a splash of cranberry juice or muddle in raspberries.
